title: Retention Check: Forward Secrecy, KDFs, and PKI

questions.q: What does forward secrecy protect that static RSA key exchange does not?
questions.a: Forward secrecy ensures that compromise of the server's long-term private key cannot decrypt previously recorded sessions. ECDHE generates independent per-session key material that is discarded after the session — no past session keys are derivable from the long-term key.

questions.q: What is the OWASP minimum cost factor for bcrypt, and what does it mean operationally?
questions.a: Cost factor 12. This means bcrypt performs 2^12 = 4,096 iterations of its internal key derivation. At this cost factor, a single hash takes approximately 100–300ms on modern hardware, making offline brute force expensive regardless of GPU resources.

questions.q: What are the three Argon2id minimum recommended parameters and what does each control?
questions.a: m=19456 (memory: 19 MB per computation, defeating GPU cracking by exhausting per-core memory), t=2 (time cost: 2 iterations over the memory block), p=1 (parallelism: 1 thread).

questions.q: Name the four checks RFC 5280 requires for certificate chain validation.
questions.a: 1. Signature validation (each cert signed by the cert above). 2. Validity period (notBefore/notAfter current). 3. Revocation status (not on CRL/OCSP). 4. Path length constraint (pathLenConstraint limiting intermediate CA depth).
